Q6.notes: Validation failed - wrong result due to floating-point precision in discount filter. Performance is good (23.7ms vs DuckDB 20ms), but correctness must be fixed first.

Q6.bottleneck: Scan/filter dominates at 16.7ms (70.5% of total time). Zone maps are working well (84.7% block skip rate). However, the scan loop is scalar - no SIMD vectorization despite AVX512 availability.
Q6.root_cause: CRITICAL: Wrong result due to floating-point precision in discount BETWEEN predicate. The code uses double comparisons (>= 0.05, <= 0.07) which don't match DECIMAL semantics. TPC-H l_discount is DECIMAL(15,2), but stored as double. Floating-point rounding causes rows with discount=0.04999... or 0.07000...1 to be misclassified.

analysis.overall: Q6 validation FAILED with wrong result (1230136229.36 vs expected 1230113636.01). Root cause: floating-point precision in discount filter - stored as double but needs DECIMAL arithmetic semantics. Performance is close to DuckDB (23.7ms vs 20ms target) but correctness takes priority. Main performance bottleneck is scan_filter (70.5%), which lacks SIMD despite AVX512 support.

critical_fixes.issue: Wrong result due to floating-point precision in l_discount BETWEEN predicate
critical_fixes.fix: Use epsilon-tolerant comparison or convert discount to fixed-point integer (multiply by 100, compare as int32_t)
critical_fixes.description: The current code compares l_discount (double) with >= 0.05 and <= 0.07. Due to floating-point rounding, values like 0.04999999 or 0.070000001 are misclassified. TPC-H specifies l_discount as DECIMAL(15,2), which has exact arithmetic. Fix: Either (1) use epsilon tolerance in comparisons (e.g., >= 0.05 - 1e-9, <= 0.07 + 1e-9), OR (2) convert discount to fixed-point integers at load time (discount * 100 → int32_t, then compare 5 <= discount_int <= 7). Option 2 is faster and enables integer SIMD.

performance_optimizations.description: The scan_filter loop (lines 174-184) processes one row at a time with scalar comparisons. With AVX512 support (confirmed in workload_analysis), we can process 8 int32_t or 4 double values per instruction. Implementation: (1) Process shipdate with _mm256_cmpgt_epi32 for 8-wide comparison, (2) Use _mm256_cmpgt_pd for discount/quantity (4 doubles at a time), (3) Combine masks with _mm256_and_si256, (4) Use _mm256_blendv_pd to conditionally accumulate revenue. Expected speedup: 3-5x on the filter loop (16.7ms → 4-6ms). Files: q6.cpp lines 174-184. Reference: knowledge/parallelism/simd.md. Note: After fixing the discount precision issue, convert to fixed-point integers to enable full int32 SIMD path.
performance_optimizations.expected_improvement: 3-5x speedup on scan_filter (16.7ms → 4-6ms), reducing total time from 23.7ms to ~12-14ms. This would beat DuckDB (20ms target) significantly.

performance_optimizations.description: Current predicate order in q6.cpp lines 176-180: shipdate, discount, quantity. According to workload_analysis.json, selectivities are: l_shipdate (0.15), l_discount (0.02), l_quantity (0.46). The most selective is l_discount (keeps only 2% of rows). Reorder to: l_discount first, then l_shipdate, then l_quantity. This minimizes subsequent predicate evaluations. With 60M rows, checking discount first eliminates 58.8M rows before the other checks. Expected improvement: 10-15% reduction in filter time (16.7ms → 14-15ms) due to short-circuit evaluation.
performance_optimizations.expected_improvement: 10-15% reduction in scan_filter time (16.7ms → 14-15ms), total time 23.7ms → 21-22ms. Small but free optimization.

performance_optimizations.technique: madvise MADV_WILLNEED prefetching for non-skipped blocks
performance_optimizations.description: Zone maps identify 92 blocks to process (out of 600). Current code uses MADV_SEQUENTIAL (line 56) which prefetches linearly, wasting I/O on skipped blocks. Optimization: After building skip_block vector (line 146), iterate and call madvise(MADV_WILLNEED) only on the byte ranges of non-skipped blocks. This prefetches ~15% of data (92/600 blocks) instead of 100%, reducing disk I/O wait. Implementation: Calculate byte ranges for each non-skipped block: start = block * block_size * sizeof(type), length = block_size * sizeof(type). Call madvise for l_shipdate, l_discount, l_quantity, l_extendedprice. Reference: storage/persistent-storage.md line 9.
performance_optimizations.expected_improvement: 5-10% reduction in scan time (16.7ms → 15-16ms) by avoiding I/O on skipped blocks. More effective on HDD (current disk_type) where random seeks are expensive.

performance_optimizations.description: The filter loop (lines 176-180) uses nested if-statements, causing branch mispredictions. With ~0.13% final selectivity (0.15 * 0.02 * 0.46), the branch predictor struggles. Convert to arithmetic evaluation: int match = (l_shipdate[i] >= min & l_shipdate[i] < max) & (l_discount[i] >= dmin & l_discount[i] <= dmax) & (l_quantity[i] < qmax); local_revenue += match * l_extendedprice[i] * l_discount[i]. This eliminates branch misprediction penalties. Expected improvement: 5-10% on scan_filter (16.7ms → 15-16ms). Reference: query-execution/scan-filter-optimization.md lines 14-23.
performance_optimizations.expected_improvement: 5-10% reduction in scan_filter time (16.7ms → 15-16ms) by eliminating branch mispredictions.

performance_optimizations.technique: Multi-column zone map - compound predicate pruning
performance_optimizations.description: Currently using separate zone maps for shipdate, discount, quantity (lines 102-106). Each block is checked independently for each predicate (lines 119-145). Optimization: Create a compound zone map that stores the conjunction result - for each block, pre-compute whether ANY row could satisfy ALL predicates. This is more aggressive than checking each predicate separately. E.g., if a block has shipdate range [1994-01-01, 1994-02-01], discount [0.05, 0.06], quantity [10, 30], we know it CAN'T satisfy the compound predicate if the ranges don't overlap. This requires storing joint statistics (e.g., min/max of (shipdate AND discount) rows). Expected improvement: Skip additional 5-10% of blocks (508 → 540 skipped), reducing scan time by 5-8%.
performance_optimizations.expected_improvement: 5-8% additional block skipping (scan_filter 16.7ms → 15.5-16ms). Requires storage schema change to add compound zone maps.

summary: Q6 iteration 0 FAILED validation due to floating-point precision in l_discount BETWEEN predicate (wrong result: 1230136229.36 vs 1230113636.01). CRITICAL FIX REQUIRED: Use epsilon-tolerant comparison or convert discount to fixed-point integers. Performance is competitive (23.7ms vs DuckDB 20ms target), but correctness must be addressed first. After fixing, primary optimization is SIMD vectorization of scan_filter (70.5% of time) for 3-5x speedup. Secondary optimizations: predicate reordering, selective prefetching, branch-free filtering. Expected post-fix performance: 12-14ms (better than DuckDB).
